description: Observer module — the observe() primitive for discovering page actions without executing
purpose: Discover available actions on a page before committing to execute them. Reduces failed clicks and wasted tokens.

observe_primitive.what_it_does: Scans the page and returns a list of interactive elements matching the instruction, WITHOUT executing any action
when_to_use: Before act() to understand what's available
When you need to choose between multiple similar elements

input.instruction: string — natural language description of what to find

elements.label: string — visible text or aria-label
elements.actionable: boolean — can this element be interacted with?
elements.confidence: number — 0-100 how well it matches instruction

page_context.title: string — page title
page_context.total_interactive: number — total interactive elements on page

snapshot.best_for: When you need to act on a specific known element

observe.best_for: When exploring or unsure what's available

observe_vs_snapshot.recommendation: Use observe() first when exploring, snapshot() when you know what you need

observe_patterns.find_buttons: observe('Find all buttons on this page')
observe_patterns.find_search: observe('Find search input or search button')
observe_patterns.find_links: observe('Find all links in the main content')
observe_patterns.find_actions: observe('What actions can I take on this page?')

integration_with_cache.description: observe() results can be cached alongside act() patterns
flow: 1. observe('Find login button') → returns [{ref: 'e42', label: 'Sign In'}]
3. Cache: {observe_pattern: 'login button', action_pattern: 'click Sign In', selector: '...'}
4. Next visit: check cache → if URL matches → skip observe, go straight to act

integration_with_planner.description: Planner can use observe() to understand page before decomposing task
flow: 2. Planner calls observe('Find all form fields')
4. Planner creates steps: fill e10, fill e11, click submit
5. Navigator executes each step with known refs

best_practices: Always observe before acting on unfamiliar pages
Use specific instructions ('Find the submit button' not 'Find stuff')
Check confidence scores — low confidence means uncertain match
Combine observe() with snapshot() for full context
Cache observe results for repeated visits
